violations.text: The hand sink at the first-floor bar was observed with a strainer in it. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Strainer was removed at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/24/2026

Cheese at the Grill Prep station cold rail was observed held at 44 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Cheese was relocated to colder refrigeration for rapid cooling at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/24/2026

Quahog stuffing and Hurricane Sauce were observed held beyond seven days form the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment must be marked with the date of preparation and held for no longer than seven days from the date of preparation unless frozen. Food items were voluntarily disposed of at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/24/2026

A scoop/spoon being used as scoops in containers of food items were observed with the handles in contact with the food itself. Utensils used to dispense food items must be stored in the food item with their handles above the top of the food within the container. Utensils were removed from foods at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/24/2026

The cold rail at the grill prep station was observed with ice being used as a cold holding aid. Per the PIC, the unit is to for service/replacement in the following week. Cold rail is not to be used for storage of food items until serviced/replaced. New Violation. Correct By: 03/24/2026

Two ice machines, several bar ice wells, and the upstairs Island Oasis machine were observed to be lacking proper air gaps. Drains to the ice machines and ice wells are to be adjusted to allow for a proper air gap. New Violation. Correct By: 04/21/2026

Employees' personal belongings were observed stored in the cookline area. Lockers or other suitable facilities shall be used for the orderly storage of employees' possessions. Items were removed and properly stored at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/24/2026
The hand-washing sink in the kitchen was lacking paper towels. PIC had a employee restocked paper towels during the time of this inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/07/2025

Insect device was observed over several food containers on a shelf. -Exposed food and food-contact surfaces must be protected from contamination by insects or insect parts. Installation of the device over food preparation areas or in close proximity to exposed food and/or food-contact surfaces could allow dead insects and/or insect parts to be impelled by the electric charge, fall, or be blown from the device onto food or food contact surfaces. PIC removed insect control device at the time of this inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/07/2025

The front door to the walk-in cooler was observed not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment/outdoor walk-in coolers must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 01/21/2025

The ice scoop handle observed in direct contact with the ice used to make customers drinks, at the bar. Proper ice scoop storage review and staff washed, rinsed and sanitized ice scoop at the time of this inspection. -During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/07/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the hood/vent in the kitchen observed with an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Non-food contact surfaces of the hood/vent must be clean as often as necessary to prevent buildup of residue. New Violation. Correct By: 01/21/2025

The sink used for food preparation is lacking an air gap. A direct connection must not exist between the sewage system and a drain originating from equipment in which food is placed. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/21/2025

Shellfish tags were not kept in chronological order at time of inspection. Shellfish tags must be maintained for 90 days in chronological order and the date when the last shellfish was used from the bag must be noted on the shellfish tag. New Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2024

Coleslaw, Tartar sauce, and cocktail sauce (made in house) were observed held at 48 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC relocated coleslaw to another cold hold unit and voluntarily disposed of sauces at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2024

A small table top cold holding well was observed in poor repair (not holding adequate ambient temperature). PIC removed all food and took unit out of service. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2024

The plumbing under the prep sink and ice machine did not have adequate air gaps. A plumbing system shall be installed to prevent backflow of a liquid or gas contaminant into the water supply by providing an air gap or installing an approved backflow prevention device. New Violation. Correct By: 03/04/2024
